summary:
Rebase task/351 onto latest main to resolve merge_ff_failed (main advanced again since step-23). task/351 has one diverging commit (9211b939 'chore(task-351): final rebase sync'). Main advanced with task-status auto-commits only (no code-file changes). Run: `git checkout task/351 && git rebase main`. Verify with `git log --oneline -3`.; Verify existing tests pass after rebase: run `cargo test -p reify-compiler recursive_detection` and `cargo test -p reify-eval recursive_unfold` to confirm both detection and eval recursive unfold tests pass on the rebased branch.; Write failing test `unfold_mutual_recursion_two_node_cycle` in crates/reify-eval/tests/recursive_unfold.rs. Build two templates: A (param n:Int=2, is_recursive=true, sub b=B(n:n-1) where n>0) and B (param n:Int=0, is_recursive=true, sub a=A(n:n-1) where n>0). Create a two-template module and eval. Assert: A.b.n == 1 (B instantiated with n=1), A.b.a.n == 0 (A instantiated with n=0), and A.b.a.b does NOT exist (guard stops at n=0). This test will FAIL against the current implementation because Phase 2 passes A's all_recursive_subs into the recursive call for B: the guard expression references A's ValueCellId keys, but local_values is built from B's value_cells, causing Undef => silent return.; Fix `unfold_recursive_sub` in crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs to handle mutual recursion correctly:
1. Change function signature: replace `all_recursive_subs: &[&'t SubComponentDecl]` and `all_recursive_sub_names: &[&str]` with `templates: &'t [TopologyTemplate]`.
2. Inside the function, compute `child_recursive_subs` from `child_template.sub_components.iter().filter(|s| s.guard_expr.is_some())` and `child_recursive_sub_names` from those subs.
3. In Phase 2 loop: iterate `child_recursive_subs` (child template's subs, not parent's). For each `next_sub`, look up the target template via `templates.iter().find(|t| t.name == next_sub.structure_name)` and pass it as `child_template` in the recursive call. Pass `templates` (not the fixed parent slice) so each depth level recomputes from its own child.
4. In Phase 3: pass `child_recursive_sub_names` to `elaborate_child_lets_only` so BFS walks the correct child-scoped sub names.
5. Update the call site (eval loop ~line 1214): remove the pre-computed `all_recursive_subs`/`all_recursive_sub_names` vectors. Pass `&module.templates` as the `templates` parameter instead.
6. Verify step-26 test now passes.; Write test `unfold_mutual_recursion_three_node_cycle` in recursive_unfold.rs: structures A->B->C->A each with param n, guards (n>0), and arg (n:n-1). With A.n=3, verify A.b.n==2, A.b.c.n==1, A.b.c.a.n==0, and A.b.c.a.b does NOT exist. This tests that the template lookup chains correctly through a 3-node cycle. Also write test `unfold_mutual_recursion_with_let_bindings`: A and B each have `let val = n * 10`. Verify that let-bindings at each mutual recursion depth are evaluated correctly (Phase 3 uses child-scoped sub names for BFS projection).; Verify step-28 tests pass. The three-node cycle test exercises template lookup across three distinct templates. The let-bindings test verifies Phase 3's elaborate_child_lets_only receives the correct child-scoped recursive_sub_names for BFS traversal.; Run full test suite for both affected crates: `cargo test -p reify-compiler` and `cargo test -p reify-eval`. Also run `cargo clippy -p reify-eval` to verify no new warnings from the signature change. Confirm all existing self-recursion tests still pass (no regression).; Rebase task/351 onto latest main and fast-forward merge. Run: `git checkout task/351 && git rebase main && git checkout main && git merge --ff-only task/351`. Verify with `git log --oneline -5 main`.
Post-escalation verification: all 31 steps already done. Task code fully merged to main (zero diff). All 12 recursive detection tests and 19 recursive unfold tests pass. No further work needed.

Completed final two steps: rebased task/351 onto latest main (step-32), then FF merged to main (step-33). All 12 recursive detection tests and 19 recursive unfold tests pass. All 33 steps now done. Task 203 complete.

Rebase task/351 onto latest main to resolve sixth merge_ff_failed. task/351 has one diverging commit (f702ef7a 'chore(task-351): complete all 31 steps, merge to main') touching only .task/plan.json and .taskmaster/tasks/tasks.json. Main advanced with 5 task-status auto-commits (no code changes). All actual code (detection tests + mutual recursion fix) is already on main via merge base d136ba49. Run: `git checkout task/351 && git rebase main`. For conflicts in .taskmaster/tasks/tasks.json: accept main's version (`git checkout --theirs .taskmaster/tasks/tasks.json`). For .task/plan.json: keep ours (the updated plan). After rebase, verify with `git log --oneline -3 task/351`.; Fast-forward merge task/351 to main. Run: `git checkout main && git merge --ff-only task/351`. Verify all tests pass with `cargo test -p reify-compiler recursive_detection` and `cargo test -p reify-eval recursive_unfold`. Verify merge with `git log --oneline -3 main`.
Post-escalation resume: verified all 33 steps done, task/351 is ancestor of main, 12/12 recursive detection tests pass, 19/19 recursive unfold tests pass. No further work needed — task is complete.
FF task/351 to main HEAD (d1c5f1ac), verified all 12 detection + 19 unfold tests pass. All 35 steps complete. Task 203 fully done.

Resolve seventh merge_ff_failed. task/351 (6f2d8201) is confirmed ancestor of main (d1c5f1ac) — all code fully merged. Main advanced with 3 task-status auto-commits (351=done, 364=blocked, 364=done). Fast-forward task/351 branch pointer to main HEAD: `git checkout task/351 && git merge --ff-only main`. Verify with `git rev-parse task/351 main` showing identical SHAs.; Verify tests pass after sync: run `cargo test -p reify-compiler recursive_detection` and `cargo test -p reify-eval recursive_unfold` to confirm all detection and mutual recursion tests pass on the synchronized branch.

Post-escalation resume: discarded stale local edit (already on main as 3c1f447b), rebased task/351 onto latest main, verified all 12 recursive detection tests + 19 recursive unfold tests pass, FF merged task/351 to main. Both branches at b8207196. All 35 steps complete. Task 203 fully done.

Resolved eighth merge_ff_failed (spurious). FF'd task/351 to main HEAD (af381339). Verified: task/351 is ancestor of main, both branches at af381339, all 12 recursive detection tests pass, all 19 recursive unfold tests pass. All 36 steps complete. Task 203 fully done.

Resolve eighth merge_ff_failed (spurious). Verified that task/351 and main both point to identical commit b8207196 — branches are already fully synchronized. No rebase or merge needed. Confirm with `git rev-parse task/351 main` (identical SHAs), `git merge-base --is-ancestor task/351 main` (true), and `cargo test -p reify-compiler --test recursive_detection_tests` (12 passed) + `cargo test -p reify-eval --test recursive_unfold` (19 passed). This merge_ff_failed was stale — main had already incorporated all task/351 commits at the time the review feedback was generated.

Post-escalation resume: all 36 steps already done. Rebased task/351 onto main (duplicate checked_neg commit dropped). FF'd to main HEAD (73898158). task/351 is ancestor of main. All 12 recursive detection tests pass, all 19 recursive unfold tests pass. Zero code diff. Task 203 fully complete — no further work needed.

Write failing test `unfold_mutual_recursion_heterogeneous_members` in crates/reify-eval/tests/recursive_unfold.rs. Build two templates with NON-OVERLAPPING member names beyond the shared param `n`: Template A has `{param n: Int = 2, param width: Int = 5, let total: Int = width + A.b.height}` with `sub b = B(n: n-1) where n > 0`. Template B has `{param n: Int = 0, param height: Int = 3}` with `sub a = A(n: n-1) where n > 0`. Both `is_recursive = true`. Eval from A(n=2). Assert: A.width == 5, A.b.height == 3, and critically: A.total == 8 (= width + A.b.height). This test will FAIL because the BFS in `elaborate_child_lets_only` (lib.rs:3644-3665) iterates `child_template.value_cells` (A's members: n, width, total) to scan entity A.b (which is a B). `ValueCellId::new("A.b", "height")` is never constructed because `height` is not in A's value_cells — so A.b.height is never projected into child_values, and A.total evaluates to Undef.; Fix BFS projection in `elaborate_child_lets_only` (lib.rs:3613-3666) to use per-entity template lookups for heterogeneous mutual recursion:
1. Add `templates: &[TopologyTemplate]` parameter to `elaborate_child_lets_only`.
2. Change BFS queue from `VecDeque<String>` to `VecDeque<(String, &TopologyTemplate)>` — each entry carries the entity path AND the template that entity belongs to.
3. Seed the queue by looking up each recursive sub's target template: for each name in `recursive_sub_names`, find the sub in `child_template.sub_components`, then find the target template in `templates` via `sub.structure_name`. Push `(entity_path, target_template)` tuples.
4. In the BFS loop body, iterate `entity_template.value_cells` (the entity's OWN template) instead of `child_template.value_cells`.
5. When enqueuing children, use `entity_template.sub_components` to find child sub names and their target templates (via `templates` lookup), instead of the static `recursive_sub_names`. This ensures each depth level uses the correct template's sub declarations.
6. Update the call site in `unfold_recursive_sub` (lib.rs:3474) to pass `templates`.
7. Verify step-37 test now passes, plus all existing mutual recursion tests still pass.; Write failing test `cyclic_let_bindings_emit_diagnostic` in crates/reify-eval/tests/recursive_unfold.rs. Build a recursive template S with `{param n: Int = 2, let a: Int = b + 1, let b: Int = a + 1, sub child = S(n: n-1) where n > 0}` with `is_recursive = true`. Eval the module. Assert: (1) the eval result's `diagnostics` contains at least one error-level diagnostic whose message contains 'circular let-binding dependency' (or similar) and names both `a` and `b`, (2) the values for S.a and S.b are `Value::Undef` (they were never evaluated due to the cycle). This test will FAIL because `elaborate_child_lets_only` currently has no cycle detection — the sorted output silently omits the cyclic nodes and no diagnostic is emitted.

Completed final 3 steps. step-40: threaded diagnostics parameter through elaborate_child_instance and elaborate_child_lets_only, added cycle detection after topological_sort that compares sorted output length against input set and emits error diagnostic naming cyclic members. step-41: verified all reify-compiler (12 detection + 12 termination + others) and reify-eval (21 recursive_unfold + all others) tests pass, clippy clean. step-42: rebased onto main and fast-forward merged successfully. All 42 steps complete.

Add cyclic let-binding detection in `elaborate_child_lets_only` (lib.rs:3682):
1. Add `diagnostics: &mut Vec<Diagnostic>` parameter to `elaborate_child_lets_only`.
2. After `let sorted_child_lets = topological_sort(...)` (line 3682), compare `sorted_child_lets.len()` against `child_let_node_ids.len()`. If `sorted_child_lets.len() < child_let_node_ids.len()`, collect the missing node IDs by computing the set difference `child_let_node_ids - sorted_set`. Extract member names from the missing `NodeId::Value(vcid)` entries.
3. Emit `Diagnostic::error(format!("circular let-binding dependency in '{}': [{}]", scoped_entity, missing_names.join(", ")))`. The entity's let-bindings involved in the cycle remain as `Value::Undef` in the snapshot (they are never evaluated, which is the existing behavior — now it's just reported).
4. Update the call site in `unfold_recursive_sub` (lib.rs:3474) to pass `diagnostics`.
5. Verify step-39 test now passes, plus all existing tests still pass.; Run full test suite for both affected crates: `cargo test -p reify-compiler` and `cargo test -p reify-eval`. Run `cargo clippy -p reify-eval` to verify no new warnings from the signature changes. Confirm all existing self-recursion, mutual recursion (homogeneous), detection, and termination tests still pass (no regression from the two fixes).; Rebase task/351 onto latest main and fast-forward merge. Run: `git checkout task/351 && git rebase main && git checkout main && git merge --ff-only task/351`. If main advanced during the operation, repeat rebase. Verify merge with `git log --oneline -5 main` and `cargo test -p reify-eval --test recursive_unfold`.
